qas.q: What are positions in the Air Force that require similar skill and qualifications?
qas.a: AFS = Air Force Specialty

qas.q: What AFS stands for munitions and weapons career fields?
qas.a: 2W

qas.q: What are the three functional areas or subdivisions?
qas.a: 2W0 munitions systems, 2W1 aircraft armament, 2W2 nuclear weapons

qas.q: What AFS loads and unloads nuclear and non nuclear munitions, explosives, and propellant devices?
qas.a: What does 2W1 aircraft armament entail?

qas.q: What AFS performs and manages several functions, such as maintenance, inspection, storage, handling, modification, accountability, and repair of nuclear weapons?
qas.a: What does 2W2 nuclear weapons entail?

qas.q:
What are the requirements to be chosen as a 2W011 helper?

Hint: 5 items, ICHieVS
qas.a: No record of emotional instability or domestic violence, normal color vision, high school or GED diploma, valid state license, secret clearance?

qas.q: What are the 5 AMMO skill levels?
qas.a: 2W011 Helper, 2W031 Apprentice, 2W051 Journeyman, 2W071 Craftsman, 2W091 Superintendent

qas.q: What are the duties of 2W011 helper?
qas.a: Successfully complete tech school.

qas.q: What are examples of non and semi skilled skill levels and skilled skill levels?
qas.a:
Non and semi skilled: 2W011, 2W031, and 2W051

Skilled: 2W071 and 2W091

qas.q: What are the requirements to upgrade to 2W031 Apprentice?
qas.a: Successfully complete tech school.

qas.q:
What are the duties of 2W031 Apprentice?

TYS
qas.a:
Work under a trainer that is a journeyman or craftsman.

Complete qualification training in Specialty Training Standard (STS) and Air Force Job Qualification Standard (AFJQS).

Learn basic munitions tasks so you can work by yourself.

qas.q:
What are the requirements to upgrade to 2W051 Journeyman?

Hint: CMRS
qas.a:
Complete CDCs.

Minimum 12 months OJT.

Signed off on all necessary core tasks in CFETP (as well as all future skill levels).

Recommended by supervisor.

qas.q:
What are the duties of 2W051 Journeyman?

Hint: WAHA
qas.a:
Work towards a CCAF degree.

Attend Airman Leadership School (ALS).

Handle tasks associated with day-to-day munitions operations. ***

Use CDCs to prepare for WAPs test. ***

qas.q: What are requirements to upgrade to 2W071 Craftsman?
qas.a:
SSgt.

12 month minimum OJT.

CAPP/AFCOMAC.

qas.q: What school is located in Beale AFB that Journeymen must attend to upgrade to Craftsmen?
qas.a: CAPP (combat ammunition and planning)/AFCOMAC (Air Force Combat Ammunition Center).

qas.q:
What are the duties of 2W071 Craftsman?

SPEN
qas.a:
Fill supervisory positions in squadron, up to group/wing level.

Plan activities.

Continue education.

NCOA when they reach TSgt.

qas.q: What are the requirements to upgrade to 2W091 Superintendent?
qas.a:
At least SMSgt.

Complete SNCOA.

Attend CAPP/AFCOMAC as a SNCO.

qas.q: What are the duties of 2W091 Superintendent?
qas.a:
Has all supervisory duties.

Can work up to the MAJCOM level.

Enforces TO compliance and government directives.

Leads and manages teams.

Ensure munitions capability, reliability, and accountability. ***

qas.q: What are the duties of 2W000 Munitions Systems Chief Enlisted Manager?
qas.a:
Top of career field ladder.

Same as superintendent but at higher levels.

qas.q: What is the requirement to be a 2W000 Munitions Systems Chief Enlisted Manager?
qas.a: Minimum rank CMSgt.

qas.q: What does AFECD stand for?
qas.a: Air Force Enlisted Classification Directory.

qas.q: What are the job descriptions in AFECD lacking in?
qas.a: The job descriptions found in AFECD are general in nature and lack unique base and MAJCOM requirements.

qas.q: What is a comprehensive education and training document which identifies life-cycle education and training requirements, training support resources, and MINIMUM CORE TASK TABLE requirements for munitions systems specialty?
qas.a: CFETP = Career Field Education and Training Plan

qas.q: What are the two parts of CFETP?
qas.a:
Part 1:
Provides necessary info for the overall management of specialty (2W0X1 skill training requirements)

Part 2: Comprehensive list of training courses and standards that support career field training requirements (STS info, course objective, support materials, training course in detail, MAJCOM requirements)

qas.q: Who determines the need to revise or change CFETP?
qas.a: AFCFM = Air Force Career Field Manager.

qas.q:
What is scheduled by the AFCFM to revise or change CFETP, and is held at AMMO schoolhouse in Sheppard AFB, TX?

What topics are discussed during this?
qas.a:
U&TW Utilization and Training Workshop.

Trends across career field, new munitions, new training requirements, and needed changes to CFETP.

qas.q: What associates degree can AMMO airmen earn with CCAF?
qas.a: Applied science degree in Munitions Systems Technology.

qas.q: What is used to identify and document individual upgrade and qualification training?
qas.a:
STS = Specialty Training Standard

STS is used to help formulate MTL by ENOIC

qas.q: What is used by Element Noncommissioned Officers in Charge to formulate their work center's Master Tasking List (MTL)?
qas.a: Primarily Part II Section A of CFETP, STS.

qas.q: What is a comprehensive list that identifies all shop training required to include deployment and unit type code (UTC) tasks?
qas.a: Master Tasking List, MTL.

qas.q:
What is a web-based training document used to track all of an individual's training records?

What did this replace?
qas.a:
TBA = training business area

Paper copies of CFETP used in conjunction with AF Form 623

qas.q: Why is it important to be familiar with all the duties of all the subdivisions of the 2W career field?
qas.a: Because they all interrelate with each other.

qas.q: Before becoming an apprentice what AFSC is assigned to Airmen in basic training?
When can an apprentice munitions troop perform a task unsupervised?
qas.a: Once trained and qualified except where prohibited by safety or security requirements.

qas.q: At what skill level can a munitions specialist expect to perform supervisory positions within the unit?
qas.a: 2W071 Craftsman ***

qas.q: What are the first steps needed prior to starting 5-skill level upgrade training?
qas.a: Complete basic training, be assigned to career field, and complete technical school

qas.q: Where can you find general skill level training requirements and career path progression information?
qas.a: Part I of the Career Field Education and Training Plan (CFETP)

qas.q: Which agency or organization develops command supplements or directives for implementation?
qas.a: Major command (MAJCOM)
